questions.text: Which of the following is the most abundant protein in the whole of the biosphere?
questions.explanation: RuBisCO is the most abundant protein in the biosphere, while collagen is the most abundant in the animal world.

questions.text: Amino acids are organic compounds containing an amino group and an acidic group as substituents on the same carbon, called the:
questions.explanation: Because they are attached to the alpha carbon, they are called alpha-amino acids.

questions.text: The bond formed between two amino acids in a polypeptide chain is a:
questions.explanation: A peptide bond is formed when the carboxyl group of one amino acid reacts with the amino group of the next.

questions.text: In a nucleic acid, a phosphate group links the 3'-carbon of one sugar of one nucleotide to the ______ of the next sugar.
questions.explanation: This forms the 3'-5' phosphodiester bond that makes up the backbone of DNA/RNA.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a polymer?
questions.explanation: Lipids have a smaller molecular weight and are not strictly polymers, though they are found in the acid-insoluble fraction.

questions.text: An enzyme that catalyzes the removal of groups from substrates by mechanisms other than hydrolysis is called a/an:
questions.explanation: Lyases catalyze the removal of groups leaving double bonds.

questions.text: The chitinous exoskeleton of arthropods is formed by the polymerisation of:
questions.explanation: Chitin is a complex polysaccharide made of N-acetyl glucosamine units.

questions.text: Proteins are heteropolymers made of ______ different types of amino acids.
questions.explanation: There are 20 standard amino acids that form proteins in living organisms.

questions.text: The helical structure of protein is stabilized by:
questions.explanation: Secondary structures like alpha-helices are stabilized by intramolecular hydrogen bonding.

questions.text: Which nitrogenous base is unique to RNA and replaces Thymine?
questions.explanation: RNA contains Uracil instead of Thymine found in DNA.

questions.text: The primary structure of a protein refers to:
questions.explanation: Primary structure is the linear sequence of amino acids in a polypeptide chain.

questions.text: Glycogen is a homopolymer made of:
questions.explanation: Glycogen is the storage polysaccharide in animals, made of glucose units.

questions.text: What is a 'prosthetic group' in an enzyme?
options: The protein part of the enzyme
questions.explanation: Prosthetic groups are organic compounds distinguished from other cofactors in that they are tightly bound to the apoenzyme.

questions.text: The non-protein part of an enzyme is called:
questions.explanation: Enzymes often require a non-protein part called a cofactor for their activity.

questions.text: Which of the following is a saturated fatty acid?
questions.explanation: Palmitic acid has no double bonds, making it saturated.

questions.text: In Watson-Crick model of DNA, how many base pairs are there in one full turn of the helix?
questions.explanation: B-DNA has 10 base pairs per turn with a pitch of 34 Angstroms.

questions.text: Competetive inhibition of an enzyme is often used in the control of:
questions.explanation: Sulfa drugs are competitive inhibitors used to kill bacteria.

questions.text: Which biomolecule acts as the 'energy currency' of the cell?
questions.explanation: Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP) stores and provides energy for cellular work.

questions.text: A nucleoside differs from a nucleotide in that it lacks a:
questions.explanation: Nucleoside = Base + Sugar. Nucleotide = Base + Sugar + Phosphate.

questions.text: Lecithin is an example of a:
questions.explanation: Lecithin is a common phospholipid found in cell membranes.

questions.text: The 'Lock and Key' hypothesis explains:
questions.explanation: It describes how an enzyme's active site fits a specific substrate.

questions.text: Which element is found in proteins but not in common carbohydrates?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen is a key component of the amino group in all amino acids.

questions.text: Cellulose is not digested by humans because we lack:
questions.explanation: Cellulase is required to break down the beta-glycosidic bonds in cellulose.

questions.text: Zinc is a cofactor for which proteolytic enzyme?
questions.explanation: Zinc is a metal ion cofactor essential for the activity of carboxypeptidase.

questions.text: Tertiary structure of protein is absolutely necessary for:
questions.explanation: The 3D folding creates active sites for enzymes and binding sites for receptors.

questions.text: The most abundant element in the human body by weight is:
questions.explanation: Oxygen makes up about 65% of human body weight, mostly due to water.

questions.text: Which of the following is a secondary metabolite?
questions.explanation: Alkaloids, flavonoids, and rubber are secondary metabolites with no direct role in primary metabolism.

questions.text: The repeating unit of nucleic acids is:
questions.explanation: Nucleic acids are polymers of nucleotides.

questions.text: Starch gives a blue color with Iodine because:
options: It has helical structure that holds I2
questions.explanation: Iodine molecules are trapped in the helical portion of amylose in starch.

questions.text: Inulin is a polymer of:
questions.explanation: Inulin is a storage polysaccharide found in some plants, made of fructose units.

questions.text: Enzymes increase the rate of reaction by:
questions.explanation: Enzymes lower the energy barrier required for a reaction to proceed.

questions.text: Which bond is found in lipids like triglycerides?
questions.explanation: Triglycerides are formed by esterification of glycerol with three fatty acids.

questions.text: What is the 'turnover number' of an enzyme?
options: Number of substrate molecules converted per unit time
questions.explanation: It indicates the efficiency of the enzyme in catalyzing reactions.

questions.text: Adult human haemoglobin consists of ______ subunits.
questions.explanation: It has two alpha and two beta subunits (quaternary structure).

questions.text: Which carbohydrate is found in the DNA backbone?
questions.explanation: Deoxyribonucleic acid contains the pentose sugar deoxyribose.

questions.text: The Km value of an enzyme is the substrate concentration at which velocity is:
questions.explanation: Michaelis constant (Km) represents affinity; lower Km means higher affinity.

questions.text: In a polypeptide, the first amino acid is called the ______ amino acid.
questions.explanation: The first amino acid has a free amino group (N-terminal).

questions.text: Sucrose is made of:
questions.explanation: Sucrose is a disaccharide commonly known as table sugar.

questions.text: Prosthetic groups are ______ part of enzymes.
questions.explanation: They are organic cofactors that are very tightly bound to the enzyme protein.

questions.text: Which of the following is an essential amino acid (must be taken in diet)?
questions.explanation: Valine is one of the essential amino acids that the human body cannot synthesize.
